requirements.summary: Fix TypeScript compilation errors preventing deployment
acceptanceCriteria: All frontend TypeScript files compile without type errors
The application deploys successfully to production with no deployment errors
All previously implemented features (Mission Dashboard, Intelligence Hub, Field Ops Briefing, Admin Panel with ban/unban system) remain functional after the fix

file_operations.description: Review and fix any TypeScript type mismatches between frontend mutation/query hooks and the backend interface defined in backend.d.ts. Ensure all backend method calls match the exact signatures in the interface, including correct parameter types (e.g., Time as bigint, Principal objects, enum values). Pay special attention to createMissionBriefing, updateMissionBriefing, createThreatAssessment, and updateThreatAssessment mutations that use complex nested types like BriefingObjective, HVTProfile, ThreatCategory, and ensure they are constructed correctly.

Verify that all mutation hooks from useQueries are invoked with correct types matching the backend interface. Check ban/unban operations use string principal text, and all asset/mission/briefing CRUD operations pass correctly typed parameters. Fix any type assertion issues or missing type conversions.

Ensure the briefing form constructs BriefingObjective and HVTProfile objects that exactly match the backend interface types. Verify missionDate is converted to bigint (Time type) correctly, and classificationLevel uses the ClearanceLevel enum properly. Fix any type mismatches in the createMissionBriefing and updateMissionBriefing mutation calls.

Fix type issues in threat assessment mutations. Ensure riskScore is passed as bigint, threatCategory uses the correct ThreatCategory variant structure, and linkedMissions is typed as Array<string>. Verify the form data transformation matches the backend createThreatAssessment and updateThreatAssessment signatures.

Check that mission creation and update mutations pass all enum types correctly (MissionStatus, ThreatLevel, MissionType) and arrays are properly typed. Ensure assignedOperatives and objectives arrays match Array<string> type requirements from the backend interface.

Verify asset profile mutations use correct enum types for ClearanceLevel, Variant_active_terminated_inactive status, and Array<SpecializedSkill> for specialization. Fix any type mismatches in createAssetProfile and updateAssetProfile calls.

Ensure the admin password verification and isCallerAdmin checks handle Promise types correctly and any async/await patterns are properly typed. Verify the actor method calls match the backend interface signatures.

Check that addMissionFieldReport mutation passes the correct parameter types (codename as string, reportContent as string) matching the backend interface. Ensure no type assertion issues exist in the report submission flow.
